title: Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)

{{Quote|Exar Kun, your dream of a Sith Golden Age is but a nightmare...from which we must now '''awaken'''.|[[Thon]]|<ref name="TSW6" />}}
The '''Battle of Yavin IV''' was one of the final conflicts of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] in [[3996 BBY]]. It culminated in the death of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and the annihilation of much of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] species on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Following the defection of fellow Sith Lord and former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and elements of the [[Republic Rim Fleet]] converged on Yavin IV—the [[Brotherhood of the Sith]]'s main staging center. In an attempt to unleash his spirit into the cosmos, Kun used a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] ritual to drain the souls of many Massassi. However, this collided with a [[wall of light]]-side energy generated by the Jedi participants, culminating in a firestorm that devastated Yavin IV's surface and destroyed much flora and fauna. Kun's spirit survived the inferno but was imprisoned within the [[Massassi Temple (disambiguation)|Massassi Temple]]s.

==Background==
[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] program of systematic galactic conquest had claimed the lives of millions, over the years that the Great Sith War burned. With the final destruction of the great [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] training headquarters at [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], it appeared that an era of self-assured complacency for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]] was now officially at an end. The need for a swift, final resolution had become all the more urgent.<ref name="TSW6" />

The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was dealt an unforeseen lucky break, however, when former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] turned away from the darkness after [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|slaying]] his [[Cay Qel-Droma|own brother]], imprisoned beneath a [[Wall of light]]. Ulic psychologically faced up to the consequences of his actions, realizing the need to undo the damage already wrought by his hand. Revealing the location of Exar Kun's main headquarters—the fourth [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the planet [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]]—the Jedi leaders under [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]] and [[Nomi Sunrider]] quickly organized the [[Republic Rim Fleet]] to take the war back to Kun.<ref name="TSW6" />

==Conflict==
[[File:YavinDevastated.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The [[Wall of light]] triggered lightning storms and forest fires all across Yavin 4.]]
Thousands of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] answered the summons, converging upon the jungle moon in a multitude of battlecraft which blotted out the sky. Looking heavenward, Kun instantly recognized Qel-Droma's betrayal, and vowed to continue the fight against the light by ''surviving''.<ref name="TSW6" /> Some ground engagements between [[Republic soldier]]s and Kun's Massassi warriors occurred.<ref name="Timeline10" /> Unable to counteract the combined might of all the Jedi, Kun summoned much of the Massassi population on Yavin IV to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] for one final ceremony. Guided by ancient Sith spellbooks, he drained the Massassi of their life-essence and used the energy in a Sith ritual to [[Transfer essence|free his spirit]] from the chains of his mortal body. For a while, it seemed that Kun would be free to ravage the cosmos forever.<ref name="TSW6" />

In response, the assembled Jedi created a massive [[Wall of light|wall of Light Side energy]] aimed at cleansing Exar Kun's corrupt presence from the galaxy and imprisoning his spirit.<ref name="Timeline10" /> However, the wall unexpectedly collided and reacted with the colossal wave of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] energy ''rising'' from Yavin—causing a chain reaction on the planet below, creating a massive firestorm unlike any ever seen before in history, utterly devouring all traces of Kun and the Massassi.<ref name="TSW6" />

With the holocaust proving to be unquenchable, the Jedi forces reluctantly departed from Yavin space, as the fires continued to rage out of control. Nomi, Ulic, Thon, and the rest of the Jedi were stunned at what they witnessed and asked themselves what the final cost of their actions would ultimately prove to be.<ref name="TSW6" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The attacking Jedi destroyed much of the life on Yavin 4, which they later worked to restore to its previous state. They thought they'd won, that they'd removed the influence of the dark side. How wrong they were. The Massassi are still here. The dark side is still present. I would not be surprised if Exar Kun's spirit remains, waiting for someone—a Jedi like myself, perhaps—to find him…|Arlo Grennen's journal|<ref name="Shadow of Revan">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Ovair_Gynt_vs_Massassi.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Master Ovair and Gynt encounter Massassi, descendants of the survivors of Kun's holocaust.]]
Two years later, Qel-Droma himself would finally comprehend the cost. His newly-christened ship, ''[[Cay's Dream]]'', landed on Yavin 4, finding a devastated world and ecosystem, with no lifesigns anywhere to be found. No trace of Exar Kun was found and Qel-Droma left. However in the darkness of the [[Massassi Temple (disambiguation)|Massassi temples]], the spirit of Exar Kun screamed futile pleas for deliverance from the darkness, damned to the hell he had made for himself, but Ulic, blind to the Force, could not hear him.<ref name="TSW6" /> Gradually over the years, Yavin IV's ecosystem regenerated and recovered till it could sustain flora and fauna again.<ref name="TOTJ:R1">[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 1]]</ref>

Some Massassi on Yavin IV, including [[Kalgrath]], also survived the firestorm.<ref name="Essential Guide to Alien Species">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref> Over the centuries, their numbers on Yavin IV multiplied. In [[3756 BBY]], these Massassi were encountered by the covert [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|True Sith]] spy [[Barel Ovair]] and the Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Eison Gynt]] who were on a mission to ostensibly eliminate the dark side energies emanating from the [[Naga Sadow's tomb|tomb]] of the Sith Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] from the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. These Massassi attacked the two offworlders and drove them deeper into the tombs where they encountered the Sith Lord's lingering energies. Seven years later, Gynt emerged wearing Massassi warrior robes and possessed by Sadow's dark side spirit. Gynt attacked master Ovair in broad daylight in Coruscant but was bested by Ovair.<ref name="Timeline7">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 7: Peace for the Republic?|int=Timeline 7: Peace for the Republic?}}</ref> In addition, there were Massassi living on other worlds including [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and [[Tund/Legends|Tund]].<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

Nearly four thousand years later in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Walter Emanus]] led a [[Corellia University/Legends|Corellia University]] archaeological expedition. However, during their excavations, they uncovered the catacombs beneath the Dark Lord's temple and reawakened his spirit. He possessed them and made them into the [[Cult of Exar Kun]], which was devoted to his worship. Under his influence, some of the cultists traveled to the [[Corellia University research outpost]] on [[Talus/Legends|Talus]] to recruit more students and researchers to their ranks. The cult based itself around the catacombs, but was destroyed when Professor [[Cornelius Wagglehorn]], a former colleague of Emanus, hired a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to drive them out. Kun himself battled the spacers, but due to his spirit form, was easily defeated and silenced once more.<ref name="Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>

[[File:ExarKunspirit_egtf.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The students of the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] face the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], four thousand years after his first demise.]]

In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Kun's temples became the site of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum]], a school where he began the re-creation of the Jedi Order. The Jedi trainees' exercises in the Force awakened Kun, who sought to subvert the Jedi training. As a spirit, Kun appeared to the young [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi apprentice]] [[Gantoris]]. The inexperienced apprentice was unable to avoid being corrupted and, when he started to resist, Kun killed him, burned from the inside. After destroying his first Jedi in thousands of years, Kun's insatiable appetite for spreading his dark wrath escalated. He was able to successfully corrupt and partly possess the young and extraordinarily talented [[Kyp Durron]].<ref name="DA" />

In addition, he sundered Skywalker's spirit from his body, demoralizing his Jedi apprentices. Kun's spirit was eventually roused from hiding and banished into [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] by the combined efforts of the Jedi apprentices, Luke's spirit, and the long-dead spirit of Kun's Master, [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]].<ref name="COTF">''[[Champions of the Force]]''</ref> After Kun's shade was vanquished, [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] destroyed [[Temple of Exar Kun|Kun's temple]] with [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], thus eliminating his dark taint.<ref name="IJedi">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>
